reasoning_model_1: Model 1 fails the fundamental requirement of Content Accuracy. The user requested the sentence 'Hey! Don't block the way, move aside!' in English. Model 1 translated this into Chinese ('Hey, bie dang lu, kao bian zhan'), violating the script adherence instruction. According to the scoring standard, if voice content is clearly inconsistent with the script, the score is 0. Although the model effectively generated the paralinguistic features (coughing, rude tone, and loud volume), the critical failure in language generation renders the audio completely incorrect for the requested text.
reasoning_model_2: Model 2 demonstrates perfect Content Accuracy, reading the English text exactly as requested. Regarding Non-Linguistic Vocalizations, it successfully inserts clear coughing sounds ('Ahem, ahem') at the beginning, warranting a high score for this dimension. However, regarding Attitude and Volume, the model performs poorly. The requested 'rude tone' is missing; the delivery sounds polite, calm, and conversational, bordering on the opposite of the requested emotion. Similarly, the 'loudly' instruction is ignored, as the speech is at a standard volume. While the coughing feature is well-executed, the emotional and volume features are insufficient.
model_comparison: Model 1 suffers from a catastrophic failure in Content Accuracy by speaking Chinese instead of the requested English text. Consequently, strictly following the scoring criteria, it receives a score of 0 across all dimensions because the content is inconsistent with the script. Model 2 maintains content accuracy and successfully produces the required non-linguistic vocalization (coughing). However, Model 2 struggles significantly with the stylistic requirements, failing to sound rude or loud. Despite Model 2's weak performance on Attitude and Volume, it wins all categories by default because Model 1 failed the primary constraint of generating the correct text.
